audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel chapter 18, first-pass restored draft
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Jonathan-David covenant, royal clothing and weapons, women's song, Saul's jealousy, harmful-spirit language, spear attacks, military reassignment, Merab and Michal as royal marriage strategy, poverty/status, genital-trophy bride-price, and source tensions.

verses.restored: When David finished speaking to Saul, life of Jonathan was bound to life of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own life.
Saul took him that day and did not permit him to return to house of his father.
Jonathan cut a covenant with David because he loved him as his own life.
Jonathan stripped robe that was upon him and gave it to David, with his garments, even his sword, bow, and belt.
David went out wherever Saul sent him and acted skillfully. Saul set him over men of war, and it was good in eyes of all people and servants of Saul.

When they came, as David returned from striking Philistine, women came out from all cities of Israel, singing and dancing to meet Saul the king, with tambourines, rejoicing, and three-stringed instruments.
Women who played answered one another and said, Saul has struck his thousands, and David his ten-thousands.
Saul became very angry, and this word was evil in his eyes. He said, They have given David ten-thousands, but to me they have given thousands. What more can he have but kingdom?
Saul watched David with suspicion from that day onward.
On next day a harmful spirit from Divine rushed upon Saul, and he raved/prophesied within house while David played with his hand as day by day. Spear was in Saul's hand.
Saul hurled spear and said, I will strike David into wall. David turned aside from before him twice.
Saul was afraid before David because YHWH was with him and had turned aside from Saul.
Saul removed him from with him and set him as commander of thousand, and David went out and came in before people.
David acted skillfully in all his ways, and YHWH was with him.
Saul saw that he acted very skillfully and dreaded him.
All Israel and Judah loved David, because he went out and came in before them.

Saul said to David, Here is my older daughter Merab. I will give her to you as wife. Only be for me a son of valor and fight battles of YHWH. Saul said, Let not my hand be against him, but let hand of Philistines be against him.
David said to Saul, Who am I, and what is my life or clan of my father in Israel, that I should become son-in-law to king?
At time Merab daughter of Saul was to be given to David, she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as wife.
Michal daughter of Saul loved David. They told Saul, and matter was right in his eyes.
Saul said, I will give her to him, and she shall become a snare to him, and hand of Philistines shall be against him. Saul said to David a second time, You shall become my son-in-law today.
Saul commanded his servants, Speak to David in secret, saying, Look, king delights in you, and all his servants love you. Now become son-in-law to king.
Servants of Saul spoke these words in ears of David. David said, Is it light in your eyes to become son-in-law to king, when I am a poor and lightly esteemed man?
Servants of Saul reported to him, saying, According to these words David spoke.

Saul said, Thus say to David: King does not desire bride-price except one hundred foreskins of Philistines, to take vengeance on enemies of king. Saul planned to make David fall by hand of Philistines.
His servants told David these words, and matter was right in David's eyes to become son-in-law to king. Days had not been fulfilled.

David arose and went, he and his men, and struck two hundred men among Philistines. David brought their foreskins, and they counted them out fully to king so he might become son-in-law to king. Saul gave him Michal his daughter as wife.
Saul saw and knew that YHWH was with David, and Michal daughter of Saul loved him.
Saul was still more afraid before David, and Saul became enemy of David all days.
Princes of Philistines went out, and whenever they went out David acted more skillfully than all servants of Saul, and his name became greatly esteemed.

translation_notes: Jonathan's love and covenant are intense political and personal bonds. The text does not require erasing queer readings, nor does it permit forcing a single modern category onto ancient relationships.
Jonathan's transfer of robe and weapons may signal affection, covenant, rank, or political recognition. It is not proof that hereditary power is privately transferable without public accountability.
Saul's confinement of David to court and repeated military appointments place a young subordinate under royal control.
The women's victory song is communal performance, not the cause of Saul's violence. Women are not responsible for male jealousy, rivalry, or abuse.
The harmful-spirit and raving/prophesying language must not stigmatize mental illness, trauma, neurodivergence, or altered states. Saul remains responsible for two spear attacks.
David's avoidance of the spear is survival under domestic and workplace violence; victims are not obligated to remain available to an abuser.
Saul offers Merab and Michal as instruments of strategy. Neither daughter is property, and the text gives no basis for assuming either woman's consent to the arrangements.
Michal's love is named, but love does not erase the royal power imbalance, the lethal bride-price, or her father's manipulation.
The foreskin bride-price is mutilation, trophy violence, and ethnicized killing. It cannot be romanticized as courtship, masculinity, covenant proof, or a sexual joke.
'Battles of YHWH' is Saul's manipulative framing of a plot to have David killed and cannot sanctify political warfare.
David's success and popular esteem do not establish moral perfection or immunity from later accountability.
safety_note: Maximum safeguards: Do not blame women for Saul's jealousy, romanticize spear attacks, diagnose distress as demonic, treat Jonathan and David as usable ammunition against queer or non-queer people, or present royal marriage arrangements as consent. Merab and Michal are not rewards or traps by nature; Saul makes them instruments. The genital-trophy bride-price is lethal mutilation and must never be celebrated, imitated, eroticized, or preached as heroic masculinity.
